aids.title: AND vs OR Memory Trick
aids.content:
**AND = ALL** - ALL inputs must be 1
**OR = ONE** - At least ONE input must be 1

Remember: 'A' for AND = 'A'LL, 'O' for OR = 'O'NE
aids.forRemembering: When AND outputs 1 vs when OR outputs 1

aids.title: The Light Switch Analogy
aids.content:
**AND = Series Switches**: Two switches in series - BOTH must be ON for the bulb to light up.

**OR = Parallel Switches**: Two switches in parallel - EITHER one ON lights the bulb.

Imagine wiring in your home!
aids.forRemembering: Physical meaning of AND and OR

aids.title: XOR = 'Different' Detector
aids.content:
XOR answers: 'Are the inputs DIFFERENT?'

0 XOR 0 = 0 (same - no)
0 XOR 1 = 1 (different - yes)
1 XOR 0 = 1 (different - yes)
1 XOR 1 = 0 (same - no)

Think: 'X marks the difference!'

aids.title: NAND and NOR Rhyme
aids.content:
NAND is NOT-AND, flip the AND,
NOR is NOT-OR, you understand.
Universal gates, they're called with pride,
Build ANY circuit, far and wide!
aids.forRemembering: NAND and NOR as universal gates

aids.title: Quick Truth Table Check
aids.content:
**AND**: Count 1s in output = 1 (only 1,1 gives 1)
**OR**: Count 0s in output = 1 (only 0,0 gives 0)
**XOR**: Count 1s in output = 2 (two differences)

Use these counts to verify your truth tables!
